Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to analyze an event log against a normative process description to identify deviations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First lets understand the normative process 1 Always starts with Receive order 2 Next is Check inventory must occur before payment authorization or fulfillment decisions 3 If inventory sufficient Receive Check Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order 4 If inventory insufficient Receive Check Cancel order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints extracted Receive order must be first Check inventory must precede Authorize payment Package items Ship order and Cancel order Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after cancellation Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close This matches the sufficient inventory path exactly No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Sequence Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Violations Package items occurs before Authorize payment The normative description says Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close the existence of both Authorize payment and Cancel order in the same case suggests a mixed path The normative text presents them as separate branches

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Ship order occurs after Cancel order This is explicitly prohibited Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 there is no Package items before Ship order The normative text says Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order So shipping without packaging is a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 for insufficient inventory after checking the inventory the process should directly proceed to Cancel order The presence of Authorize payment between Check and Cancel violates this direct progression

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close This matches the insufficient inventory path exactly No violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 5 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Missing Package items The normative text says Once the payment is authorized the items should be prepared for shipment Package items Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Violating Cases 2 3 and 5 Case 1 and 4 are compliant
